title: index.ts imports the `sqlite` package, which is not a dependency
evidence: src/index.ts:2 `import { open } from 'sqlite'`; package.json lists `sqlite3`/`@types/sqlite3` but not `sqlite` (the `"sqlite"` in package.json is a keyword, not a dep). `tsc` cannot resolve the module.
suggestion: Add `sqlite` to dependencies, or rewrite the DB layer against the raw `sqlite3` API.

title: server.ts imports non-existent ./database and ./routes/books modules
evidence: src/server.ts:2-3 import `./database` and `./routes/books`; no src/database.ts and no src/routes/ directory exist in the tree. This file (the only one with `export default app`) cannot compile.
suggestion: Create the missing modules, or delete server.ts and export the app from index.ts.

title: Acceptance suite book.api.test.ts fails to load (wrong import path)
evidence: src/__tests__/book.api.test.ts:2 `import app from './src/index'` resolves, from src/__tests__/, to the non-existent src/__tests__/src/index; jest reports the suite as a failed load and its 2 tests never execute. index.ts also has no default export, so even the corrected path (`../index`) would import `undefined`.
suggestion: Fix the import to `../index` (or `../server`) and add `export default app` to the target module so supertest receives the Express app.

title: Stored test_coverage=1.0 is a false positive — only a trivial placeholder test runs
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=1.0, but the only test that actually executes is src/__tests__/basic.test.ts:5 `expect(1).toBe(1)`. The scorer's pass-rate regex counts jest's `Tests: 1 passed, 1 total` line and misses the suite-level load failure of book.api.test.ts, so a broken project scores as fully covered.
suggestion: Treat this run as failing the test gate despite the stored score; no behavior of the service is actually verified.

title: R12: fewer than 3 substantive tests actually run
evidence: Three `it` blocks are authored (basic.test.ts:1, book.api.test.ts:2) but the 2 real API tests never load (see test-1); only the trivial placeholder executes. how_to_verify requires >=3 tests that exist AND run.
suggestion: Repair the acceptance suite so the health/create/list/filter/update/delete tests run, giving at least 3 passing behavioral tests.

title: P1 (ATDD prompt): acceptance suite is not executable, so it cannot drive or demonstrate the requirements
evidence: The ATDD prompt requires executable acceptance tests exercised through the public HTTP interface. book.api.test.ts uses supertest at the HTTP boundary (correct style) but never loads (test-1), covers only /health and POST /books (no list/filter/get-by-id/update/delete/validation scenarios), and shares a persistent ./bookstore.db rather than starting from an empty service per test.
suggestion: Fix the import wiring, add one client-level acceptance scenario per requirement, and isolate each test with a fresh in-memory DB.

title: Two competing, unreconciled app entrypoints
evidence: src/index.ts (all routes, no export, listens on 3000) and src/server.ts (export default app, incomplete imports) both define an Express app; neither is a clean, importable, buildable entrypoint and package.json `main` points at dist/index.js.
suggestion: Consolidate to a single app module that exports the Express instance and a separate thin entrypoint that calls listen().

title: ?author= filter uses substring LIKE match rather than exact match
evidence: src/index.ts:47 `SELECT * FROM books WHERE author LIKE ?` with `%${author}%` — a query for author 'Lee' also returns 'Leeson'. Reasonable UX but a deviation from an exact-match filter.
suggestion: Confirm the intended semantics; use `author = ?` for exact filtering if required.
